title: Variables 1
description: In Rust, variables are created with the `let` keyword. This exercise asks you to fix a variable binding.
instructions: Make the code compile by fixing the variable declaration.
hints: In Rust, you need the `let` keyword to create a variable binding.
The syntax is: let variable_name = value;
explanation: In Rust, variable bindings are created with the `let` keyword. Unlike some languages, you must explicitly declare variables before use. This helps prevent typos and makes code more readable.
